title: phoebe excelsa
intro: Phoebe excelsa, also known as the East Indian tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Lauraceae, native to the Himalayan foothills and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of P. excelsa include the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating fever and digestive problems. The herbal preparations of P. excelsa include tea, decoctions, and infusions, which can be made by boiling the leaves and bark in water to extract its medicinal properties.
